items.q: What distinguishes gamma rays from X-rays of the same frequency?
items.correct: Origin — gamma rays come from nuclear processes; X-rays come from electron transitions or deceleration of charged particles.
items.misconception: Gamma rays have higher frequency than any X-ray.
items.random_wrong: Gamma rays are slower than X-rays.
items.why: Students distinguish bands by frequency exclusively, missing that gamma and X-ray bands overlap in frequency and are classified by origin.

items.q: What is the difference between the activity of a source and the dose received by a person?
items.correct: Activity (Bq) is the rate of nuclear decays in the source; dose (Sv) measures the biological effect of absorbed radiation on tissue.
items.misconception: Activity and dose are the same — both measure how dangerous the radiation is.
items.random_wrong: Activity is for nuclear power stations and dose is for medical X-rays.
items.why: Students conflate 'how much radiation is emitted' with 'how much radiation a person absorbs', missing the role of distance, shielding and tissue sensitivity.

items.q: After one half-life, what happens to an individual radioactive nucleus?
items.correct: It has a 50 percent chance of having decayed — radioactive decay is random for any single nucleus.
items.misconception: Every nucleus is guaranteed to have half-decayed after one half-life.
items.random_wrong: The nucleus shrinks to half its original size after one half-life.
items.why: Students treat half-life as deterministic for individual atoms, when it is a statistical statement about large populations of nuclei.

items.q: Capacitance of parallel plate capacitor depends on:
items.why: It's Area, separation, permittivity. “Charge stored only” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Permittivity of free space epsilon0 mantissa (x10^-12 F/m, to 2 decimals)?
items.why: Exact answer: 8.85 x10^-12 F/m. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
